title: glossorhyncha myrtillus
intro: Glossorhyncha myrtillus, also known as the Beech Tree Moss or Myrtle Moss, is a type of lichenized fungus that grows on the trunks of beech trees, typically in temperate forests. The medicinal properties of Glossorhyncha myrtillus lie in its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Glossorhyncha myrtillus can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to be consumed, or used topically in the form of a salve or ointment.
